changes: OpenClaw posted v2026.4.19-beta.1 at 2026-04-19 02:01 UTC and v2026.4.19-beta.2 at 2026-04-19 05:55 UTC, while the main repo reached roughly 360.2k stars in the 2026-04-19 scan.
Hermes Agent crossed the 100k-star line in the 2026-04-19 scan while its latest release remained v2026.4.16.
ZeroClaw shipped v0.7.3-beta.1051 at 2026-04-19 03:49 UTC and then promoted that burst into stable v0.7.3 at 2026-04-19 03:55 UTC.
The tracked set was tightened around canonical repos: Hermes on NousResearch/hermes-agent, TinyClaw on TinyAGI/tinyagi, and HiClaw on agentscope-ai/HiClaw.
OpenClaw showcase evidence expanded the use-case log with home automation from discovered controls, scheduled visual briefings, and accounting intake workflows.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw itself is moving again today, which is a stronger signal than ecosystem-only motion.
Hermes crossing 100k stars makes it a heavyweight adjacent benchmark rather than a niche nearby project.
ZeroClaw is showing that the lightweight Rust branch can turn rapid prerelease cadence into stable releases quickly.
Canonical repo cleanup reduces blind spots in the watch and keeps the atlas aligned with where real development now lives.
The new showcase examples broaden the proof set from chat assistance into repeatable workflows and ambient daily interfaces.
